relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks refuge into the newly divided land.
The cities promised through Moses now become actual places.
One who kills by mistake may flee from revenge long enough for judgment.
At the gate, the fugitive must speak, and elders must make room.
Hot blood is not allowed to outrun truth about intent.
The person remains in protected distance until judgment and the high priest's death open return.
Kedesh, Shechem, and Hebron are set apart in the western hills.
Bezer, Ramoth, and Golan answer them east of Jordan.
Israelite, sojourner, and resident alien receive the same right to flee and stand before judgment.

contemplative_note: Joshua 20 is one of the book's clearest checks on retaliatory violence. The land must contain places where panic pauses, anger cannot instantly kill, and immigrant and native-born alike receive a hearing before the community.
